results.title: Daemons - Charlie Labs
results.content: # Daemons. Understand what Charlie daemons are, how they wake, and how DAEMON.md controls behavior. **Human readers:** the fastest way to get started with daemons is to work directly with Charlie.**Charlie can help you** suggest where to start and add your first daemon. Use the prompts below to ask Charlie in Slack, GitHub, or Linear. Explain what daemons are and how they work.Explain what daemons are and how they work. Daemons are repo-defined operating roles for Charlie. A daemon lives at: `.agents/daemons//DAEMON.md` The daemon file tells Charlie:. * what `watch` conditions or `schedule` can wake it. If you are a human reader, the intended workflow is to work with Charlie. * explain how to use daemons. Use the daemon CLI when you want the quickest path to browse examples, inspect an example before adapting it, scaffold a draft into a repository, or validate local daemon files. * **Unix daemons:** a Charlie daemon is a repo-authored operating role, not a long-running OS process.

results.title: Agents create work, Daemons do the rest | Charlie Labs
results.content: # Agents create work, Daemons do the rest. Daemons are always-on AI processes that work proactively across Slack, Linear, GitHub, and more. Defined with simple .md files, daemons work 24/7 without explicit instruction so your team can focus on novel work. ## Daemons are proactive and always-on. *Self-initiated work that doesn't wait for a prompt.*. Daemons work when you specify roles, goals, and outcomes — not individual tasks or TODOs. Just add a daemon to a repo and the whole team wins every day forward. ---name:  bug-triagepurpose:  Ensures every bug is complete, prioritized, assigned, and root-cause-analyzed.watch: - when a Linear issue is created with the bug label - when the bug label is added to a Linear issueroutines: - set priority based on Sentry impact data and issue context - assign the issue to the owner determined by CODEOWNERS - append missing context to the issue body - post a root cause analysis comment with supporting evidencedeny: - create or delete Linear issues - add or remove labels on Linear issues - override priority that was set by a human - open pull requests - modify source code or configuration files --- ## Policy - Only fill in what is missing.

results.title: "Agents create work. Daemons maintain it." Who are ... - note
results.content: Daemons maintain it." Who are "Daemons" by Charlie Labs, which launched a new category overseas? On April 21, 2026, Charlie Labs announced a new product category called **Daemons** on Show HN and their company blog. This article organizes what Daemons are and how they differ from existing agent frameworks, based on the official website (charlielabs.ai) and the poster's comments on the Show HN thread. The Charlie Labs blog post "Introducing Daemons" encapsulates the positioning of this new category. In the Show HN post (account name: rileyt), the background is explained that Charlie Labs itself released Daemons as a result of a pivot from the autonomous coding agent path. The most distinctive feature of the Daemons implementation idea is that **each daemon is defined in a Markdown file at .agents/daemons//DAEMON.md**. Daemons maintain it."** The sentence can also be read as a foreshadowing that the next point of discussion in the agent era will be "what to maintain." It is a product worth keeping an eye on in the context of 2026 agents, including how the boundaries with existing agent frameworks, Claude Routines, and GitHub Actions-like automation will shift.
